# Hypotheses and Research Questions

## Primary Hypothesis

<!--
State your main hypothesis clearly and testably, e.g.:

"Individuals who convert from NL to MCI exhibit measurably different 
neuropsychological, demographic, and behavioral profiles at baseline 
compared to those who remain cognitively stable."
-->

## Secondary Hypotheses

<!--
H2: Memory-related features (LOGIMEM, MEMUNITS, CRAFTDVR) contribute 
    the most discriminative power for NL → MCI classification.

H3: APOE ε4 carrier status adds significant predictive value beyond 
    cognitive test scores alone.

H4: Disease regression (MCI → NL) is associated with milder baseline 
    impairment and absence of depressive or apathetic symptoms.
-->

## Operationalization

<!--
Define precisely:
- How you define "NL → NL", "NL → MCI", "MCI → NL", etc.
- Which visit pairs are compared (consecutive visits? first and last?)
- What constitutes a "group" in your classification task
- How you handle patients with multiple transitions
-->

## Expected Outcomes

<!--
What do you expect to find? This section helps frame the later discussion 
of whether hypotheses were verified or falsified.
-->
